content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

1. What is the Current Market Regime?
Neutral regime — normal volatility at 28.0% with mixed breadth (3/8 sectors positive). Market lacks directional confirmation; breadth skews weak despite pockets of strength in Energy and Heavy Industry.

2. Which sectors are leading?
Energy & Chemicals ABOVE TREND: PETKM (1W: +14.46% | 1M: +4.52%) and TUPRS (1W: +5.57% | 1M: +17.14%) driving sector outperformance; Heavy Industry RECOVERY/ABOVE TREND: ASELS (1W: +2.09% | 1M: +15.09%) and TOASO (1W: +3.62% | 1M: -9.48%) showing selective strength.

3. Which sectors are lagging?
Banking & Finance BELOW TREND: VAKBN (1W: -8.70% | 1M: -20.07%) and HALKB (1W: -17.75% | 1M: -16.29%) lead the downside; Retail & Food BELOW TREND: SOKM (1W: -7.76% | 1M: -22.34%) is weak and weighing on market breadth.

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

4. Top Performing Stocks?
PETKM (1W: +14.46% | 1M: +4.52%) ABOVE TREND. SASA (1W: +8.85% | 1M: -11.19%) RECOVERY. TUPRS (1W: +5.57% | 1M: +17.14%) ABOVE TREND. BIMAS (1W: +5.44% | 1M: -3.91%) RECOVERY. SISE (1W: +5.03% | 1M: -12.83%) RECOVERY.

5. Worst Performing Stocks?
HALKB (1W: -17.75% | 1M: -16.29%) BELOW TREND. VAKBN (1W: -8.70% | 1M: -20.07%) BELOW TREND. SOKM (1W: -7.76% | 1M: -22.34%) BELOW TREND. ULKER (1W: -6.84% | 1M: -19.47%) BELOW TREND. TRMET (1W: -6.74% | 1M: +3.41%) CORRECTION.

6. Momentum Stocks?
Highest 1W movers: PETKM (1W: +14.46% | 1M: +4.52%) ABOVE TREND, SASA (1W: +8.85% | 1M: -11.19%) RECOVERY, TUPRS (1W: +5.57% | 1M: +17.14%) ABOVE TREND, BIMAS (1W: +5.44% | 1M: -3.91%) RECOVERY, SISE (1W: +5.03% | 1M: -12.83%) RECOVERY.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

7. Banking Sector Outlook?
Banking is under pressure and BELOW TREND across major names: AKBNK (1W: -1.69% | 1M: -15.31%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -1.84% | 1M: -14.14%) BELOW TREND, ISCTR (1W: -2.24% | 1M: -18.85%) BELOW TREND, YKBNK (1W: -2.76% | 1M: -14.66%) BELOW TREND, HALKB (1W: -17.75% | 1M: -16.29%) BELOW TREND, VAKBN (1W: -8.70% | 1M: -20.07%) BELOW TREND.

8. Industrial Sector Outlook?
Heavy industry mixed: ASELS (1W: +2.09% | 1M: +15.09%) ABOVE TREND and TOASO (1W: +3.62% | 1M: -9.48%) RECOVERY provide selective long ideas, while EREGL (1W: -2.11% | 1M: -4.65%) BELOW TREND and FROTO (1W: -0.44% | 1M: -16.90%) BELOW TREND signal lingering weakness.

9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?
Energy is the market's engine with TUPRS (1W: +5.57% | 1M: +17.14%) ABOVE TREND and PETKM (1W: +14.46% | 1M: +4.52%) ABOVE TREND; SASA (1W: +8.85% | 1M: -11.19%) RECOVERY and SISE (1W: +5.03% | 1M: -12.83%) RECOVERY show commodity-driven rebounds, while TRMET (1W: -6.74% | 1M: +3.41%) CORRECTION highlights divergence within the group.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

10. Portfolio Recommendation?
Neutral regime — prefer tactical overweights in Energy & Chemicals and selective Heavy Industry longs (TUPRS, PETKM, ASELS) while keeping exposure to beaten-up banks and retail underweight until breadth improves; size positions for intra-week momentum with tight stops given mixed signals.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?
TUPRS (1W: +5.57% | 1M: +17.14%) ABOVE TREND for continuation vs profit-taking. PETKM (1W: +14.46% | 1M: +4.52%) ABOVE TREND for follow-through on momentum. ASELS (1W: +2.09% | 1M: +15.09%) ABOVE TREND for defense order news sensitivity. HALKB (1W: -17.75% | 1M: -16.29%) BELOW TREND for volatility and potential forced flows.

12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?
Energy & Chemicals for earnings/cost cues and momentum extensions. Banking & Finance for risk-off flow and liquidity-driven moves. Heavy Industry for selective rebounds, especially defense and autos.

13. Key Levels & Triggers?
Watch XU100 reaction at recent 50-day resistance and month-to-date support levels; a sustained break above 50-day would validate the energy-led rally while a move below 1-month lows would deepen the banking-led correction. Key catalysts: CBRT communications, Q1 corporate earnings, global risk sentiment and commodity price moves.

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Neutral market with normal volatility (28.0%) and mixed breadth (3/8 sectors positive). Energy & Chemicals lead and warrant tactical overweight, banks and retail remain clear underweights; monitor XU100 technicals and upcoming macro/corporate catalysts for regime shift.
